name: I was partly at fault in my work fall in Murray, can I still get comp?

mainEntity.name: I was partly at fault in my work fall in Murray, can I still get comp?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes, workers' comp ignores your fault level.[2]
acceptedAnswer.description:
**Yes, workers' compensation is no-fault, so your partial responsibility doesn't bar benefits.**[2]

Unlike personal injury suits, workers' comp doesn't reduce or deny claims based on employee fault unless intoxication or intentional self-harm.

- Focus on work-relatedness, not blame.
- Employer negligence irrelevant for comp eligibility.
- Comparative fault applies only to third-party claims.[3]

Report promptly regardless. This protects your medical and wage benefits fully.

description: Yes, workers' comp ignores your fault level.[2]
